notes.introduction: An exposition of Genesis 14 on the name El Elyon (Most High God), tracing the encounter between Abraham and Melchizedek and the revelation that God is possessor of heaven and earth. Part of a series on the names of God, with supporting references from Exodus 9, Deuteronomy 32, 1 Corinthians 10, and Psalms 57, 83, and 91.
themes: god as possessor of heaven and earth

sections.heading: Review of Elohim and Series Context
